title: The City Of New York Vs Homer Simpson

jokes.text: 91% of all traffic accidents are caused by you six guys
jokes.setup: Police statistics about traffic accidents in general
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific statistic that these exact six bar patrons cause almost all accidents
jokes.explanation: Excellent specificity and absurdity - the idea that these exact six guys cause 91% of all accidents is perfectly ridiculous while feeling character-appropriate

jokes.text: I knew we could do it!
jokes.setup: Being told they cause almost all traffic accidents
jokes.punchline: Treating this terrible statistic as an achievement to be proud of
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment for Barney - his oblivious pride in destructive behavior is both funny and character-consistent

jokes.text: We'll choose the same way they pick the pope. Everybody reach in and draw a pickled egg. Whoever gets the black egg stays sober tonight
jokes.setup: Needing to choose a designated driver in a fair way
jokes.punchline: Using papal selection method but with pickled eggs instead of ballots
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of the sacred papal conclave with bar culture. The specificity of pickled eggs makes it uniquely Moe's

jokes.text: No!
jokes.setup: Drawing the black egg that makes him designated driver
jokes.punchline: Homer's anguished cry upon realizing he can't drink
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat - Homer's despair is character-appropriate and sets up the episode's premise

jokes.text: Hey, everybody, I'm Peter Pants-less!
jokes.setup: Barney getting drunk while Homer must stay sober
jokes.punchline: Declaring himself 'Peter Pants-less' - a nonsensical drunk alter ego
jokes.explanation: Classic Barney drunk behavior - the alliteration and nonsensical nature fits his character perfectly

jokes.text: Hey, it's Duffman... the guy in a costume that creates awareness of Duff
jokes.setup: Duffman's entrance as a corporate mascot
jokes.punchline: His awkwardly corporate self-description that breaks the fourth wall
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of corporate mascots who openly admit they're just marketing tools - meta and character-specific

jokes.text: I can't! I'm the designated driver!
jokes.setup: Duffman offering Barney free beer and everyone chanting for him to drink
jokes.punchline: Barney's anguished refusal despite being an alcoholic
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff - Barney's alcoholism makes his adherence to designated driver rules both admirable and tragic

jokes.text: Duff wholeheartedly supports the designated driver program
jokes.setup: Barney refusing free beer because he's the designated driver
jokes.punchline: Beer company mascot suddenly supporting responsible drinking
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of corporate double-speak - beer companies promoting responsible drinking while actively trying to get people drunk

jokes.text: No. The Playboy Mansion. Playboy Mansion! Shut up! It's my car, and I say we're going to the lost city of gold
jokes.setup: Drunk patrons arguing about where to go
jokes.punchline: Escalating from realistic locations to completely mythical destination
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from plausible drunk destinations to completely impossible ones - captures drunk logic perfectly

jokes.text: That's just drunk talk. Sweet, beautiful drunk talk
jokes.setup: Barney wanting to go to the lost city of gold
jokes.punchline: Homer dismissing it as drunk talk while simultaneously romanticizing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer contradiction - he knows it's nonsense but finds it beautiful anyway. Great character consistency

jokes.text: Just slide it under the door
jokes.setup: Homer asking Barney to bring his car back tomorrow
jokes.punchline: Casually suggesting an impossible method of car return
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd throwaway line delivered with complete casualness - the impossibility makes it funnier

jokes.text: All I remember about the last two months is giving a guest lecture at Villanova. Or maybe it was a street corner
jokes.setup: Barney trying to remember what happened during his blackout
jokes.punchline: Starting with something prestigious then revealing it might have been completely different
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection structure - starts dignified then deflates completely. Great insight into Barney's confused drunk memories

jokes.text: Marge, you can stand there finding fault, or you can knit me some seat belts
jokes.setup: Marge expressing concern about Homer's homemade car
jokes.punchline: Homer suggesting knitted seat belts as a solution to safety concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - completely missing the point while offering a hilariously inadequate solution. Perfect character voice

jokes.text: Nothing good has ever come out of New York City
jokes.setup: Getting a letter from New York City
jokes.punchline: Homer's sweeping, ignorant dismissal of an entire major city
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer prejudice and small-town thinking - sets up his NYC phobia perfectly

jokes.text: Yeah. That's what people do in Russia
jokes.setup: Lisa saying you can't judge a place you've never been to
jokes.punchline: Homer's completely nonsensical comparison to Russia
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer non sequitur - his reasoning makes no sense but he delivers it with complete confidence

jokes.text: I was on my way to the Harrisburg Coat Outlet to buy an irregular coat
jokes.setup: Homer beginning his NYC flashback story
jokes.punchline: The specificity of going all the way to Harrisburg for discount irregular clothing
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - Homer would absolutely travel far for cheap irregular clothing. Great specificity

jokes.text: And that's when the CHUDs came at me
jokes.setup: Homer's traumatic NYC flashback
jokes.punchline: Referencing the 1980s B-movie monsters as if they're real NYC residents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reference that shows Homer's warped perception of NYC through B-movie lens. Perfect mix of pop culture and character

jokes.text: if you only focus on the pimps and the CHUDs
jokes.setup: Homer's traumatic story about NYC
jokes.punchline: Marge treating fictional movie monsters as normal NYC residents alongside actual problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that treats Homer's absurd perspective as normal. Marge's deadpan delivery makes it even funnier

jokes.text: Nine bucks? This one's on me!
jokes.setup: Family excited about cheap bus fare to NYC
jokes.punchline: Homer's false generosity over tiny amount
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer cheapskate humor but works for character

jokes.text: But you're not gettin' this!
jokes.setup: Agreeing to go to New York
jokes.punchline: Homer protectively clutching something (implied to be his wallet or valuables)
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy beat showing Homer's paranoia about NYC - character-appropriate fear response

jokes.text: Dad, our baby pictures were in there
jokes.setup: Homer disposing of something before the trip
jokes.punchline: Revealing he threw away irreplaceable family memories out of paranoia
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy about Homer's priorities - he'd rather lose family memories than risk NYC thieves getting them

jokes.text: How come we had to transfer in Atlanta twice?
jokes.setup: The family arriving in New York by bus
jokes.punchline: The illogical routing that required multiple transfers in the wrong direction
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about airline/bus routing inefficiency - relatable travel frustration

jokes.text: I just think we should've paid the extra $1.50 and gotten a bus with restrooms
jokes.setup: The uncomfortable bus journey
jokes.punchline: Revealing they chose an even cheaper option without basic amenities
jokes.explanation: Good setup of the family's extreme cheapness - $1.50 being too much for basic human comfort

jokes.text: Hey. Marge, I can't feel my legs. I can't feel my legs! Homer! Homer, those legs belong to the man behind you.
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about losing feeling in his legs
jokes.punchline: He was feeling someone else's legs the whole time
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with nice misdirection and payoff

jokes.text: Homer, those legs belong to the man behind you
jokes.setup: Homer panicking about not feeling his legs
jokes.punchline: He was touching someone else's legs the whole time
jokes.explanation: Classic cramped travel comedy with added awkwardness of Homer unknowingly molesting a stranger

jokes.text: Wow! I feel like such a nobody
jokes.setup: Seeing New York City for the first time
jokes.punchline: The overwhelming feeling of insignificance in the big city
jokes.explanation: Relatable first-time NYC experience expressed with Lisa's characteristic introspection

jokes.text: You guys rock! - Eh, maybe a little
jokes.setup: Bart enthusiastically telling ZZ Top they rock
jokes.punchline: The band member's modest, understated response to being called rock gods
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan response that subverts expectations - famous rock stars being casually modest

jokes.text: So if anyone asks, we're sophisticated millionaires from the Ozarks
jokes.setup: Warning the family about NYC criminals
jokes.punchline: The oxymoronic cover story that makes no sense geographically or culturally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - combining sophistication with the most backwoods region possible. Brilliantly contradictory

jokes.text: 'Cause once the sun goes down, all the weirdos turn crazy.
jokes.setup: Homer setting meeting time in Central Park
jokes.punchline: His paranoid view that weirdos get worse at night
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer paranoia about urban dangers

jokes.text: Hey, immigrants, beat it! Country's full!
jokes.setup: Seeing the Statue of Liberty
jokes.punchline: Homer yelling anti-immigration sentiment at the symbol of American immigration
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire - Homer missing the irony of his location while expressing nativist views

jokes.text: Okay, folks. You heard the lady. Back into the hold. We'll try Canada
jokes.setup: Homer yelling at immigrants
jokes.punchline: Ship captain actually following a random tourist's anti-immigrant rant
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that takes Homer's rant to its logical absurd conclusion - authority figure taking him seriously

jokes.text: Hey, when you're done with that, I got something up here you can bite on!
jokes.setup: Car boot/clamp biting Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude sexual innuendo directed at inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crudeness - not particularly clever but character-appropriate inappropriate response

jokes.text: Hey, why don't you be polite, you stinkin' pus bag!
jokes.setup: Someone telling Homer to be polite
jokes.punchline: The hypocritical delivery with an extremely rude insult
jokes.explanation: Good ironic structure - asking for politeness in the rudest possible way. Classic NYC attitude stereotype

jokes.text: Your plea has been- Rejected
jokes.setup: Automated system asking Homer to plead not guilty
jokes.punchline: Immediately rejecting the plea without consideration
jokes.explanation: Good bureaucracy satire - the illusion of choice immediately removed. Perfectly captures frustrating automated systems

jokes.text: That's- How many hours? Ten, 11, denominator-
jokes.setup: Homer trying to calculate hours between 9am and 5pm
jokes.punchline: His mathematical confusion leading to completely wrong terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer stupidity - simple math becomes impossible, ending with random math vocabulary

jokes.text: Uh, okay. Just send it to: Jesus, care of the Pentagon
jokes.setup: Homer offering to write to the homeless man
jokes.punchline: The completely nonsensical mailing address combining religion and military
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd address that combines the sacred and military in an impossible way - perfectly random yet memorable

jokes.text: but unlike yourselves, I was born without taste buds. Let me demonstrate
jokes.setup: Bart panhandling on the subway
jokes.punchline: Using a fake disability as his panhandling gimmick
jokes.explanation: Good Bart scheming behavior - creative fake disability for panhandling shows his entrepreneurial spirit

jokes.text: So hungry. Can't leave car.
jokes.setup: Homer has been waiting by his car for hours
jokes.punchline: His caveman-like speech showing desperation
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's degradation through simplified speech patterns

jokes.text: No bowl. Stick. Stick
jokes.setup: Homer asking for a bowl of Khlav Kalash
jokes.punchline: The insistence on serving it only on a stick despite it being inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Great immigrant vendor stereotype with the absurd insistence on stick delivery regardless of practicality

jokes.text: Mmm! Now, what do you have to wash that awful taste out of my mouth?
jokes.setup: Homer eating the terrible Khlav Kalash
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic 'mmm' followed by immediate need for relief
jokes.explanation: Good setup for next joke while showing Homer's politeness despite disgust

jokes.text: Oh, geez! I'll take a crab juice
jokes.setup: Choosing between Mountain Dew and crab juice
jokes.punchline: Homer actually choosing the impossible disgusting option
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff showing how much Homer hates Mountain Dew that he'd choose the impossible alternative

jokes.text: Don't even think about it, pal!
jokes.setup: Homer desperately needing bathroom, looking at storm drain
jokes.punchline: Cop preventing him from using public space as bathroom
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's bathroom crisis - cop reading his desperate thoughts perfectly

jokes.text: No. No, Lisa. They're just sleeping... upside down and inside out
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if the dead rabbits in Chinatown are dead
jokes.punchline: Marge's gentle lie that makes no anatomical sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parental euphemism that's more disturbing than helpful - perfectly captures Marge's protective instincts

jokes.text: Oh, no! Chinese fire drill! Serious this time!
jokes.setup: Bart causing fireworks incident in Chinatown
jokes.punchline: Treating real emergency as if it's just a game
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Bart's casual reaction to chaos he caused

jokes.text: Frontsies. Backsies. I'm with them. V.I.P. coming through. Very important person. Give me frontsies
jokes.setup: Homer desperate to use the bathroom, cutting in line
jokes.punchline: His childish terminology and increasingly desperate lies
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Homer's desperation through increasingly childish behavior and transparent lies

jokes.text: I certainly hope someone stabs him in the eye
jokes.setup: Homer rudely cutting in line
jokes.punchline: The polite British accent delivering an extremely violent wish
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between polite British delivery and shocking violent content - great cultural stereotype subversion

jokes.text: We're buying our sign on the installment plan
jokes.setup: Bart asking if this is MAD Magazine when sign says 'MAD'
jokes.punchline: Explaining they can't afford the full magazine name on their sign
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of magazine industry financial problems - buying signage on installment is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: Get me Kaputnik and Fonebone! I wanna see their drawings for the 'New Kids on the Blech.'
jokes.setup: Showing Bart what really goes on at MAD Magazine
jokes.punchline: Made-up MAD-style names and their parody of New Kids on the Block
jokes.explanation: Good MAD Magazine pastiche with appropriately terrible pun names and celebrity parody

jokes.text: Wow! I will never wash these eyes again
jokes.setup: Seeing the inner workings of MAD Magazine
jokes.punchline: Bart's overwrought declaration about preserving the memory
jokes.explanation: Good Bart enthusiasm and tendency toward dramatic overstatement - character-appropriate reaction

jokes.text: Wow! I will never wash these eyes again.
jokes.setup: Bart witnessing the real MAD Magazine office
jokes.punchline: His extreme reaction treating it as sacred experience
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's reverence for MAD Magazine

jokes.text: When I grow up, I wanna be in the Betty Ford Center
jokes.setup: Watching a musical about the Betty Ford Center
jokes.punchline: Lisa's innocent aspiration to be in drug rehabilitation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about childhood innocence - Lisa completely missing what the Betty Ford Center is for

jokes.text: Brain, how can I ever thank you? Just don't bump me on your way out of the car
jokes.setup: Homer having an idea to solve his parking problem
jokes.punchline: Talking to his brain as a separate entity with physical concerns
jokes.explanation: Great Homer weirdness - treating his brain as a roommate he might physically disturb. Perfect character voice

jokes.text: Die! Die!
jokes.setup: Homer attacking the boot on his car
jokes.punchline: His violent rage against inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy but fits Homer's frustration escalation

jokes.text: Homer one, New York nothing!
jokes.setup: Successfully removing the parking boot
jokes.punchline: Framing his small victory as winning against the entire city
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer grandiosity - turning a minor parking violation resolution into epic victory against metropolis

jokes.text: My God! That maniac is headin' straight for us! Go, Secretariat! Go!
jokes.setup: Homer driving recklessly toward people
jokes.punchline: Treating a car like a racehorse with a famous horse name
jokes.explanation: Good absurd panic response - treating car escape like horse racing with specific famous horse reference

jokes.text: Trust me. Throw the kids. No time for the baby.
jokes.setup: Homer urgently loading family into car
jokes.punchline: Suggesting abandoning baby to save time
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Homer's skewed priorities under pressure

jokes.text: Trust me. Throw the kids. No time for the baby
jokes.setup: Homer urgently trying to get his family in the car
jokes.punchline: Escalating from throwing children to abandoning the baby entirely
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation comedy - Homer's panic leading to increasingly inappropriate child endangerment suggestions

jokes.text: We'll see, honey. We'll see
jokes.setup: Lisa asking if they can come back to New York next year
jokes.punchline: Homer's non-committal response after his traumatic experience
jokes.explanation: Standard parental deflection after traumatic family experience - captures Homer's exhaustion and reluctance
